title: Medical Examiner Rules Alex Pretti's Death a Homicide; DHS Announces Body Cameras
excerpt: The Hennepin County Medical Examiner ruled the January 24 death of ICU nurse Alex Pretti a homicide by multiple gunshot wounds. In response to mounting scrutiny, DHS Secretary Noem announced all ICE and CBP officers deployed to Minneapolis would be equipped with body-worn cameras.
content:
On February 2, 2026, the Hennepin County Medical Examiner released findings ruling the death of Alex Pretti — shot by federal agents on January 24 — a homicide. His cause of death was 'multiple gunshot wounds' and he was recorded as having been 'shot by law enforcement officer(s).'

The homicide determination came as DHS's own preliminary review of the Pretti shooting contradicted the Trump administration's initial narrative about the incident. Video footage showed Pretti was disarmed by one officer before a second agent fired.

  • ProPublica had reported the identities of the agents who fired as Border Patrol agent Jesus Ochoa and CBP officer Raymundo Gutierrez.
  • The DHS report to Congress stated two agents fired at Pretti, but it was unclear whether one or both struck him.
  • In response to the homicide rulings in both the Good and Pretti cases, DHS Secretary Noem announced all officers deployed to Minneapolis would be equipped with body-worn cameras — a policy previously lacking for ICE deportation officers.
  • Immigration advocates noted the killings had placed the U.S. on pace to surpass the number of ICE-related deaths in 2025.
mediaPlaceholder: Hennepin County Medical Examiner official report on Alex Pretti's cause and manner of death
